activity: The next destination on your grand tour of the antipodes is Sydney. Spend a few nights here exploring the historical Rocks District, littered with boutique shops, cafes and wine bars or head to the beautiful botanical gardens full of wild flora and fauna. During your time in the cosmopolitan city of Sydney we have included an iconic cruise around the harbour. Explore hidden bays and coves as you enjoy this scenic cruise allowing you to discover the city from a different angle. activity: From the palm trees and golden beaches of Queensland, fly west to the arid plains of the Red Centre and the monolithic rock known as Uluru. The outback is where you will witness true Aboriginal sentiment. Learn about the cultural significance of Ayers Rock on a guided tour around the base of Uluru or head to the skies and enjoy a scenic flight over neighbouring Kata Tjuta. Enjoy an iconic Table 131 dinner under the stars at Uluru where you will enjoy a traditional Australian feast accompanied by a didgeridoo performance (included). The perfect way to end your outback experience. More Experiences in This Area: - Outback Survival — Darwin, Northern Territory, Australia — Outback Survival

activity: Dive straight into the outback to kick-off this awesome adventure. Flinders Ranges gives you the most awe-inspiring scenery. Explore the vast gorges and valleys, it is a wildlife lovers dream as you try to spot Red Kangaroos, Emus, Euros, Yellow-footed Rock Wallabies. Staying at the earthy and rustic-chic Arkaba Homestead, you will get a taste of classic Australian country hospitality with a few ‘Wild Bush Luxury' touches. With over 60,00 acres being shared by just 10 guests, set in a classic flinders building with thick stone walls, deep shady verandas and a corrugated roof. There is so much to do here, bush-walking, photography, Aboriginal rock art or just soaking up the sights and sounds of outback Australia from the gorgeous swimming pool. 4WD wildlife drives are definitely on the cards here, drive through the dramatic scenery, spot kangaroos, wallabies and wombats and enjoy a picnic lunch overlooking the endless panorama. Take to the skies for an exclusive and memorable experience with an epic helicopter flight over the vast outback, take in the compelling geology and ever-changing palette of the Flinders Ranges and devour the delectable canapes and sundowners as the sun sets over the endless landscape before you. This really is the best start to your journey. activity: Take in the vast Australian scenery as you travel south to awesome Adelaide Hills. Mount Lofty House is a glamorous, boutique retreat nestled in this famous and spectacular wine region. You will be in enchanted luxury from the moment you arrive. Devour a gorgeous meal at their multi award-winning restaurant and relax with a tipple at the cocktail bar. The Stables Day Spa and outdoor swimming pool offers the best anecdote for some relaxation after your trip before you set off on your tour of the vineyards. Whilst at Mount Lofty House, make sure to take part in the range of optional activities on offer to you here, from privately guided wine and gourmet food tours; hikes along the Heysen walking trail; cycling through the Adelaide Hills; golf at the nearby Mount Osmond Golf Club; or day trips to the Cleland Wildlife Park, home to both kangaroos and koalas. We highly recommend the Ultimate Adelaide Hills tours, home to some of South Australia's best wines and gourmet hotspots. Spend a day exploring the charming towns, vineyards and cellar doors of the Adelaide Hills in the company of your expert private guide. Relish your time as you travel through valley visiting a number of prime vineyards and sampling the delicious wine and equally as good food as you go. activity: Next you will fly across to Kangaroo Island, which is located just 15 km off the coast of South Australia. Kangaroo Island boasts outstanding natural beauty and its relative isolation has ensured an abundance of wildlife in a stunning and protected natural environment. Explore the island's natural wonders including secluded cliffs overlooking a rugged coastline, views of the wild Southern Ocean and Kangaroo Island's sugar-white beaches not to mention the huge variety of endemic wildlife that flourish here. activity: Leaving the wilds of Kangaroo Island behind you, leap to the dynamic and cosmopolitan city of Sydney. Staying at the uber-chic Park Hyatt Sydney, a boutique feel hotel with internationally acclaimed Hyatt service - a great combination. This is THE ultimate address in Sydney, as you wake up to panoramic views of the Opera House and the sparkling waters of the Harbour. The rooftop pool is the perfect place to cool down with a cocktail in hand after a day of exploring Sydney's sights. You will have plenty of time to explore this awesome city with its trendy waterside restaurants and bars. To really get under the skin of this city e have included a unique tour to see Sydney from a different perspective. The full-day private tour will take you completely off the well beaten tourist trail (although you will see all of the main attractions too!) and see Sydney through the eyes of a local. Exploring in a unique vintage car, this tour teaches you about the hidden secrets, the wonderful people, local cafes and secluded harbour front lookouts. You will get to experience the Sydney that other tourists just don't get to see! Bring out the surfer in you as you vitis Bondi Beach. This bespoke private day tour will showcase the other side of this understated area. Of course, no trip to Bondi would be complete without the customary surfing lesson at the renowned surf lifesaving club so you'll begin this tour with a private lesson with a local surf instructor before exploring the lesser known side of Bondi. Followed by a walking tour of Bondi showing you the other side of this famous suburb. Wander the funky back streets, visit some of the local art galleries or cafés that this hipster area is home too. You might like to spend some time visiting the local artisanal shops or opt for a more scenic coastal walk seeing Bondi from a different perspective the choice is yours. activity: After a relaxing stay in Sydney, continue to Lord Howe Island, just a short flight from Sydney, where you are transported to a real ‘treasure island', a paradise lost in time and perfectly preserved. Capella Lodge rests atop romantic Lovers Bay, at the foot of the imperious mountain twins rising dramatically above. Ooozing luxury boutique charm, the beach house style and relaxed, luxe-inspired ethos makes for a stunning retreat. A stunning turquoise lagoon stretches out in front of the lodge's indoor and outdoor lounging areas and the clever architectural design means that the floor to ceiling windows, open plan bar, lounge and restaurant really let the outside in - this is somewhere to truly escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. With its unspoilt beaches and lack of development or pollution, this really is a rare gem of a destination. Snorkel in the pristine waters or trek overland in the dense native forest. An adventure lover's playground with just 400 non-residents allowed on the island at any one time, if you're seeking remoteness, luxury and the chance to connect with nature, then Lord Howe island is the island for you. During your time at the luxurious Capella Lodge, enjoy the eleven beautiful beaches that make up the area, as well as exploring the coral reef beyond. Glass bottom boat tours run regularly, as well as snorkelling trips with green sea turtles and visits to the mighty Balls Pyramid. Equally, divers will love uncovering these biodiverse waters around the Malabar Range, the Admiralty Islets and within the sheltered lagoon. On land, there are also plenty of expertly guided walks to enjoy including a climb up to the summit of Mount Gower. A pampering awaits at the Capella Spa; choose from the refreshing Capella Signature or for something a little different, the Sacred Stone Therapy will leave you truly rejuvenated. Elsewhere, enjoy a round of golf or a game of tennis or simply relax by the pool. tour_name: A Day in the Swan Valley
location: Morning: Perth to Guildford and the Swan Valley
activity: Head east on Guildford Road from Perth to the capital’s oldest inland town, Guildford. Stop at the Swan Valley Visitor Centre to pick up a free map of the Swan Valley Food and Wine Trail – a 32-kilometre loop taking in over 200 attractions. Get your morning sugar hit with a visit to some of the Swan Valley’s makers of fine sweet treats, from chocolate, nougat and nuts, to ice creams and sorbets. If you have a designated driver for the day, let them take you cellar door hopping from boutique family-run vineyards and organic wineries to large-scale premium wine producers. Many of the wineries offer alfresco dining beside the vineyards, pairing their creations with the finest of Western Australia’s fresh produce. Appreciators of hand-crafted beers and cider will also find plenty to tempt their palate at the region’s boutique breweries, many offering light bites and hearty fare for a lazy lunch in the beer garden. Or opt to fill a picnic basket with gourmet goodies and fresh produce from the road-side stalls and stores, then take your pick from ten local picnic spots, parks and reserves in and around Guildford and the Swan Valley region.

tour_name: A Day in the Swan Valley
activity: The Swan Valley is a hotspot for creativity in arts and crafts too. With many galleries and workshops along the Food and Wine Trail, it’s a good place to pick up unique pieces by local sculptors, painters, potters, printmakers and woodworkers. If you are travelling with young children, there are plenty of fun ways to spend an afternoon, including the Swan Valley Heritage Cycle Trail, Caversham Wildlife Park, The Maze - Home of the Outback Splash Family Fun Park and many more. To return to Perth, head south through the Swan Valley, on West Swan Road or the Great Northern Highway to Guildford and retrace your tracks west along Guildford Road.

activity: Fremantle is 30 minutes by car (via the Stirling Highway or Kwinana Freeway and Canning Highway) or 30 minutes by train from Perth City. Or, you can take it slow with a Swan River cruise from Barrack Street Jetty to Fremantle Port. On arrival, grab a sidewalk table on Fremantle's bustling ‘Cappuccino Strip’, ponder the long list of coffee options and do some people-watching. Or go in search of some of Fremantle’s delectable baked goods for breakfast or brunch. Take a wander through Fremantle’s maze of streets, filled with Georgian and Victorian-era architecture, where Fremantle’s independent fashion designers flaunt their unique styles. With its free-spirited ‘anything goes’ attitude, 'Freo' (as it's commonly referred to by locals) is also a mecca for artists from the worlds of music, performing arts, painting, Aboriginal art and sculpture. Explore the local galleries, check out the annual festivals and see what’s on at the Fremantle Arts Centre. If you’re in Fremantle on a Friday, Saturday, Sunday (or even Monday if it's a public holiday), step into the iconic Fremantle Markets. For over 100 years, its hall has been filled with exotic aromas, fresh produce, fashion, live music, performance art, crafts and Aboriginal art.

location: Lunch: Fishing Boat Harbour and Micro-Breweries
activity: Freo is the home of handcrafted beer in Western Australia. Many of the local brews have gained a dedicated following in the eastern states too. These brew houses also offer some great beery bites and a lively, family-friendly atmosphere for a long lunch. Alternatively, you can feast on fresh seafood, fish and chips at Fremantle's Fishing Boat Harbour. It’s been in operation since the early 1900s and is still the best place to get the catch of the day.

activity: Hit the history trail to Victoria Quay. The modern architecturally-designed Western Australian Maritime Museum creates a striking setting for some of the most awe-inspiring vessels in maritime history. Here, you can step inside an Oberon-class submarine and see the yachting icons Australia II and Parry Endeavour suspended from the ceiling. Or head for the Roundhouse, Western Australia’s oldest public building, and the World Heritage-listed Fremantle Prison. After 136 years of continuous use, the prison opened its imposing gates to the public in 1992 to share its gripping stories. Choose from day tours, haunting night tours, or adventurous tunnel tours. If the beach is more your scene, take your pick from Fremantle’s four hotspots. The local favourites are Port Beach and Leighton Beach – much-loved for their white sand, swimming and snorkelling spots. Windsurfing and kitesurfing are also popular when the afternoon breeze gets up. Or, for more retail therapy, venture a little further afield to the George Street Precinct in East Fremantle and you’ll discover a quieter enclave of historic charm, arts, antiques and cafes. Make the 30-minute journey back to Perth via the Fremantle train line or by car on the Canning and Kwinana route.

location: Morning: Breakfast & the Heart of Mandurah
activity: Kick off your day at Wood and Stone Café, winner of the 2025 Gold Plate Award for Best Breakfast, or head to Turquoise Café Mandurah at the Mandurah Ocean Marina for their iconic breakfast tower. After breakfast, wander the Marina and snap a photo at the heart sculpture, celebrating Mandurah's Aboriginal name, Mandjoogoordap, and its cultural heritage.

activity: Jump aboard a dolphin cruise for a one-hour boat tour of Mandurah's waterways. Experienced guides share stories of local history, the estuary and the resident dolphins. Enjoy fish and chips on board or hire your own boat to cruise to one of the many waterside restaurants at Mandurah Ocean Marina. Families can also choose a pirate-themed cruise for a fun, kid-friendly experience.

activity: Head to the Mandurah Visitor Centre for local advice, maps and bookings. Pick up an e-bike or e-scooter and cycle along the coastal path to visit Santi Ikto at Halls Head, overlooking the Indian Ocean. Stop at Boundary Island Brewery for a refreshment, then continue to Yaburgurt Cirkelstone Giant by the Peel-Harvey Estuary, home to abundant birdlife and neighbouring the Creery Wetlands. Wildlife enthusiasts can finish at Melros Reserve in Dawesville to see kangaroos grazing. End with a picnic at Blue Bay Beach and watch the sun set over the ocean. Make your 45-minute journey back to Perth.

activity: Get the perfect introduction to Perth by viewing its iconic cityscape from one of the largest inner city parks in the world – Kings Park and Botanic Garden. Catch the free Perth Central Area Transit (CAT) bus, make the ten-minute drive, or take the 25-minute walk from the centre of Perth. A visit to the park between August and September adds a profusion of colour to the view, with an extraordinary display of Western Australian wildflowers. Lunch in Kings Park, or head back to Perth and discover the hidden gems of the laneways – from quirky cafes, to cool small bars and restaurants.

activity: Head to Northbridge and Perth Cultural Centre, home to the Art Gallery of Western Australia, Western Australia Museum, State Library and Perth Institute of Contemporary Arts. From here, you can also follow the City Public Art Walk to view a series of art installations. Alternatively, spend your afternoon seeking out your style of retail therapy by heading to King Street for high end labels and boutiques, Watertown for outlet shopping, or the Hay and Murray Street malls for local and global brands.

activity: Perth’s award-winning restaurants plate up the finest fresh local produce with premium wines and many have views to die for. Check out the Australian Good Food Guide for dining options. If you book your table early, you’ll have time to catch a live performance at Perth Concert Hall, State Theatre or His Majesty’s Theatre.
